title: What a D3 College Football Gameday is like #collegefootball #nfl #football
body: A lot of times with D3 schools, you just have to be one of the better players on your team and you’ll probably get picked up by your local program. In my hs years we got like 8-10 players per year going to one of our 3- division 3 programs we have in my town.

title: What a D3 College Football Gameday is like #collegefootball #nfl #football
body: As a D2 player in the mid 80s, we took a bus ride from Portland, OR to Ogden, Utah to play Weber St. After the game, we drove all the way back to Portland overnight. By the time we got close to Portland the next morning, the team comedians were clucking like chickens at each other while the head coach in the front seat had this thousand yard stair, like he’d given up. Too funny!!!!!😂
author: @EOW-nv1nc

title: What a D3 College Football Gameday is like #collegefootball #nfl #football

title: What a D3 College Football Gameday is like #collegefootball #nfl #football
body: i was a manager for my D3 football team and its exactly like this, i was the guy who recorded the film they used for film studies so it was interesting to travel with the team during the long ones. Plus side is that i get my own bed at the hotel (not my own room tho, i had to share room with the other manager) and i didnt travel on the bus, we drove a separate equipment van

title: Virtual Campus Tour 2020 | University of North Dakota
body: I go to UND. Honestly THE best college campus I've seen. Old school Harry potterish vibes in a way, and modern as can be at the same time.
author: @james_votava

title: Virtual Campus Tour 2020 | University of North Dakota
body: I've been fortunate enough to visit a lot of great college campuses (Notre Dame, Army/West Point, Princeton, Colgate, Williams, Lehigh, Lafayette, Bucknell, Georgetown, Cornell, Yale, Penn, Delaware, Michigan, Villanova the list goes on) and the University of North Dakota stacks up really well with the best of them; well laid out campus, great landscape with beautiful consistent architecture. Not hating on NDSU but UND's campus is considerably nicer than their rival to south. Grand Forks is also a really cool small city/fringe college town. If the winters weren't SO extreme in Grand Forks in the winter I think the school would have a wider appeal. As it is, a real sleeper school for those who don't mind -20 to -30 temps....
author: @tuowl0564

title: Virtual Campus Tour 2020 | University of North Dakota
body: Awesome video and campus. I could hear deafeaning silence in those hallowed halls. I was hoping to see the School of Aeronautics. Wish I could have attended UND as an international student.
author: @tickedoffsheikh8587

title: Virtual Campus Tour 2020 | University of North Dakota
body: Great job with the video.  I attended UND from 1990-1993 and it's nice to see all of the improvements that have been made to all of the buildings and facilities.  The new Student Union looks like an exciting addition when it opens Fall 2021.  I find it funny that you call the office "One Stop" within Twamley.  We used to call it "the Twamley Shuffle" (this is pre-internet, mind you), as it referred to walking from office to office for hours on end trying to get all of your university business completed at Twamley.    Too bad we couldn't get a peak at the world class Aerospace facilities in this video!  Keep up the good work on the campus, and I wish you the best.
author: @Jetfuture737
